headline: Live Where Brookline Began
subheadline: A rare residential collection in the historic heart of Brookline Village

heroStats.label: Year Established
heroStats.detail: One of Brookline's earliest planned neighborhoods

heroStats.label: Lot Size
heroStats.detail: Among the largest private lots in Brookline Village

heroStats.label: Historic Landmark
heroStats.detail: Recognized for architectural and historical significance

points.heading: Irreplaceable Location
points.description: Steps from Brookline Village's shops, restaurants, and the MBTA Green Line. Direct access to Longwood Medical Area, Kenmore, and downtown Boston.

points.heading: Rich Heritage
points.description: Built in 1843 as part of Brookline's first garden suburb, 19 Linden Street is the best-preserved example of the original Greek Revival homes designed for Boston's merchant class.

points.heading: Rare Scale
points.description: At nearly half an acre, this is one of the few remaining large lots in Brookline Village — offering space, privacy, and light that smaller urban parcels cannot match.

points.heading: Top-Tier Schools & Community
points.description: Brookline Public Schools consistently rank among the best in Massachusetts. The neighborhood offers a walkable, community-oriented lifestyle minutes from Boston.

scenarios.name: Heritage Collection
scenarios.description: 12-14 luxury residences blending preserved Greek Revival character with contemporary design. The restored 1843 landmark anchors a curated residential enclave.
scenarios.targetBuyer: Professionals, medical faculty, downsizers seeking character and convenience

scenarios.name: Boutique Adaptive Reuse
scenarios.description: The original Greek Revival house transformed into 2-3 signature residences, with a new 10-12 unit building set behind — respecting the streetscape while maximizing the deep lot.
scenarios.targetBuyer: Design-conscious buyers seeking one-of-a-kind homes with historic provenance
